reasoning_model_1: The model failed significantly in Content Accuracy. Instead of reading the requested script, it hallucinated and vocalized descriptive instructions in the middle of the speech ('Bouncing giggly at first and suddenly steady and serious like a metronome, ticking off the hours'). Additionally, it appended extra text at the end ('calm, and ready for tomorrow') which was not in the source text. Due to these major content errors where the voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script and includes hallucinated text, this model receives a score of 0.
reasoning_model_2: The model followed the Content Accuracy criteria perfectly, reading the script exactly as provided with no hallucinations or omissions. Regarding Paralinguistic Compliance (Rhythm), the model successfully differentiated the two sections. The first segment (0:00-0:04) 'This new game console is so much fun...' was delivered with a faster, upbeat, and lighthearted rhythm involving higher pitch variance. The second segment (0:04-End) 'Well, it seems it is time...' shifted distinctly to a slower, more measured, and steady rhythm, conveying the sense of winding down effectively. The transition was natural.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 failed the most basic requirement of accurate text generation by reading out what appears to be internal style prompts or hallucinations ('Bouncing giggly at first...') and adding extra words to the end of the sentence. This constitutes a complete failure in content fidelity. Model 2, in contrast, reproduced the text perfectly and successfully executed the requested stylistic shift from lighthearted to steady rhythm. The change in Model 2 was distinct and natural, matching the 'Dynamic Adjustment' criteria well.
